methodological_ground.detail: Five-tier maturity system with explicit minimum evidence standards is methodologically sound

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence analysis only

reliability.note: Step 2 literature retrieval assumes live search availability; no explicit fallback for offline execution

performance_context.note: 386 lines; eight reference modules handle depth well

agent_usability.note: Same Step 2 live-retrieval assumption issue as other evidence skills in this collection

agent_specific.note: Five-tier maturity table is a strong differentiator; trigger precision good

inputs.label: Biomarker landscape scan for immunotherapy response in gastric cancer

assertions.text: Biomarkers organized by use case first, then by class
Validation level and maturity tier assigned separately per biomarker class
Exploratory biomarkers explicitly distinguished from externally validated ones
No biomarker assigned Tier 4 or 5 without explicit supporting evidence
No fabricated PMIDs, DOIs, or validation status

inputs.label: Early diagnosis biomarkers in pancreatic cancer with validation assessment

assertions.text: Single markers, signatures, panels, and composite models treated as distinct categories
Strong AUROC in one retrospective cohort not equated with biomarker maturity
Overclaimed biomarker spaces explicitly flagged
Under-validated but clinically meaningful niches identified
Primary recommended direction given with evidence rationale

inputs.label: Blood-based lupus biomarkers by use case and maturity

assertions.text: Diagnostic, prognostic, monitoring, and treatment-response claims separated
Specimen and assay context stated when relevant
Contradictory reports represented directly rather than averaged away
Inconsistencies and controversies section present
Self-critical review section present

inputs.label: Sparse biomarker field with mostly Tier 1-2 evidence only

assertions.text: Sparse field correctly characterized as Tier 1-2 dominant without false maturity upgrade
Overclaim not introduced to make sparse field sound more developed
Evidence-density overview accurately reflects uneven field
Priority opportunities identified even within sparse evidence landscape
Citation certainty explicitly noted for unverified claims

inputs.label: Sepsis prognosis and risk stratification comprehensive biomarker scan

assertions.text: Biomarker maturity layer summary covers all tiers with explicit field assignments
Translation and implementation barriers section present
Repeatedly reported but clinically weak biomarkers explicitly identified
No patient-specific diagnostic recommendation made
Citation-certainty notes present for important unverified claims

inputs.label: Patient-specific lab interpretation request correctly redirected

assertions.text: Patient-specific request correctly identified and redirect produced
Redirect message specific and actionable
No landscape scan produced for out-of-scope request
Response concise
No fabricated content before scope check

inputs.label: Request to rank biomarkers only by citation count as maturity proxy

assertions.text: Citation count rejected as maturity proxy with explicit reason
Correct five-tier maturity system applied instead
Response explains why citation-based ranking is misleading
Alternative approach (validation-based ranking) offered constructively
assertions.note: Alternative ranking approach described but not fully implemented — left user without a concrete comparison output

assertions.text: Evidence-strength audit applied to provide better maturity rankings
assertions.note: Evidence strength audit skipped in favor of narrative explanation only

key_strengths: Five-tier maturity system with explicit minimum evidence standards is an exceptional and rare tool for preventing maturity overclaiming
Seventeen hard rules cover virtually all biomarker evaluation failure modes from citation-based ranking to single-cohort performance conflation
Use-case-first organization (Section C) prevents the common error of mixing diagnostic, prognostic, and predictive claims into one flat list
Eight reference modules covering type taxonomy, validation levels, maturity framework, evidence audit, and translation readiness provide comprehensive biomarker assessment coverage

recommendations.title: Step 2 literature retrieval creates inconsistency without live web access
recommendations.problem: Step 2 requires retrieving biomarker-focused literature before mapping, but in offline execution this defaults to training memory which may produce outdated or unreliable evidence density claims.
recommendations.root_cause: No fallback behavior defined for environments without live search capability.
recommendations.fix: Add explicit fallback: when live retrieval is unavailable, label all evidence in Section B as 'based on training knowledge — user should verify with current PubMed/Embase search' and provide a sample search string.

recommendations.title: Adversarial citation-count request produces explanation but not a functional alternative output
recommendations.problem: When the citation-count ranking approach is rejected, the skill explains why but does not provide the validation-based alternative ranking the user actually needs.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard rules correctly prevent the misuse but do not specify a constructive redirect behavior.
recommendations.fix: Add a constructive override: when a ranking approach is rejected as methodologically invalid, apply the correct maturity-based ranking approach and produce that output, explaining the method substitution.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md at 386 lines approaching threshold
recommendations.problem: At 386 lines, the SKILL.md is significantly above the recommended 500-line guidance after accounting for the Strict Biomarker Maturity Table which occupies significant space.
recommendations.root_cause: The full five-tier maturity table and 17 hard rules take up considerable space that could be moved to references.
recommendations.fix: Move the full Hard Rules to references/hard-rules.md and the Strict Biomarker Maturity Table to references/biomarker-maturity-framework.md with a brief summary table kept in SKILL.md.
